author: @zenbudhism
text: About three times more efficient than photo voltaic solar cells

author: @sveindanielsolvenus
text:
Do I understand correctly that here you are using the heat pipe that you made in a previous video, meaning a sealed copper pipe with water and graphene suspended inside, and then you have put that pipe inside a what? Did you say solar tube?
Is that the same as this one: http://www.electric-bike-kit.com/solar-tube.aspx ?

But in the description you say it is not even in a vacuum, so I am confused.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +sveindaniel that is right - the commercial tube is a vacuum tube but the heat pipe itself isn't under vacuum

author: @bridgendesar
text: What do you think about this http://www.gizmag.com/graphene-coated-condensers-power-plant-efficiency/37804/?utm_source=Gizmag+Subscribers&utm_campaign=9e1980ae52-UA-2235360-4&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_65b67362bd-9e1980ae52-90065713

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: bridgendesar looks intersting

author: @GreenmeResearch
text:
Imagine putting the Thermocell in a vacuum tube, which a clever guy converted into a solar stove/gril instead of using it to heat water.
http://www.gosunstove.com/

A friend introduced me to Lovell's website and I saw how he imagined stacking those plates up in an insulated shell with very good results in the 100 celcius range.
You will get triple that using vacuumtubes.

Would be a fun project to laminate the layers in rod forms and put it out to test.
I don't know of a better way to convert light to heat to electricity.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Greenme Research nice idea - gives me a thought

author: @myrussia2760
text: Robert, I have been looking at these solar glass vacuum tubes from China for heating water, they claim to reach temperatures close to 200c and with a cooper heat tube inside the head of such a tube you can easily put a TIG generator to it, plus supply your hot water needs.... Just a thought

author: @dash-4150
text: I would use emergency pressure releif valves of course

replies.author: @dash-4150
replies.text: I thought about taking a pressure cooker, and converting it into a high output percolater.  Then push the high temp water n steam through a n air cooled radiator before returning the water to the pressure cooker.

replies.text: That should have good works for a thermal pump, and heat transfer to aid in capture in the heat released by a wood stove/ fire place
